ralph_core_truths.what_is_it: Ralph Workflow is the operating system for autonomous coding: a free and open-source composable loop framework and AI orchestrator.
ralph_core_truths.who_is_it_for: Developers and technical teams doing ambitious software work that benefits from a structured workflow instead of a chat session.
ralph_core_truths.why_different: It keeps a simple Ralph-loop core, then composes that core into planning, development, verification, and broader workflow loops with strong defaults.
ralph_core_truths.why_now: You can use the default workflow as-is today, or build your own workflow on top without giving up control of your tools or process.

discovered_candidates.name: OvernightAgent
discovered_candidates.why: Queue-based CLI for unattended overnight runs against claude/codex/opencode. Produces SUMMARY.md with verification results. Four-gate verify pipeline, 36 typed events, daemon mode.
discovered_candidates.differentiation: Queue-based (throughput) vs RalphWorkflow's spec-driven loop (finish-state trust). Planning loop validates spec before execution. Composable loop framework. Reviewable finish state as primary output.

market_validation.what: Real-world postmortem: 12 multi-agent coordination bugs surfaced across a single autonomous-overnight cycle. Failures include: usage limits mid-task requiring manual continue, recursive hooks with no escape, silent data gaps that auditor agents called 'all good', structural false confidence from multi-session audit overlap.
market_validation.why: Every failure mode maps directly to bounded-task design, finish receipts, and clean re-entry points that Ralph Workflow provides. Months of audit results structurally false → fresh-session-per-phase model catches this. Agent ran out of per-session usage mid-task → bounded-task model prevents runaway. Recursive hook → explicit stop conditions.
market_validation.use_in_content: Blog post: '12 Multi-Agent Bugs in One Night — What Ralph Workflow Gets Right'

market_validation.source: Cemri et al., MAST paper (arXiv:2503.13657), cited via futureagi.substack.com
market_validation.what: 1,600+ annotated execution traces across 7 multi-agent frameworks. 14 failure modes in 3 categories. 41-86.7% multi-agent failure rate. Cohen's Kappa 0.88 agreement — most rigorous taxonomy of multi-agent failure available.
market_validation.why: Directly validates that multi-agent system failures are structural, not accidental. Ralph's bounded-task, single-agent-per-phase model avoids the 14 failure modes at the architecture level.
market_validation.use_in_content: Cite MAST taxonomy in positioning content about why Ralph's architecture reduces failure surface

market_validation.source: Anthropic, April 2026 research preview (via chatforest.com, claudeapi.com)
market_validation.what: Cloud-hosted automation on schedule/API/GitHub event triggers. Combined with Auto Mode (March 24 GA, 30-60 min unattended runs) moves Claude Code from interactive tool to always-on dev infrastructure.
market_validation.why: Biggest category shift seen in weeks. Anthropic building cloud-hosted unattended pipeline layer natively. Ralph's local-first, agent-agnostic positioning becomes more differentiated (not cloud-locked, not Anthropic-dependent) but category window is compressing fast.
market_validation.use_in_content: Messaging emphasis: Ralph works with any agent CLI, keeps code on your machine, produces something you can actually inspect

market_validation.what: PR review time spikes 91% between month 3 and month 9 of AI code adoption. Structural coupling of AI-generated code increases over time.
market_validation.why: Directly validates Ralph Workflow bounded-scope, reviewable-finish thesis with real data. The more AI code you generate, the harder it gets to review — Ralph's plan→verify loop is designed for exactly this problem.

market_validation.what: Research review on converting autonomous coding agent benchmark gains into maintainer-accepted production impact using merge-grounded evals, transcript checks, and emotionally legible collaboration behavior.
market_validation.why: The 'merge-grounded' terminology converges with Ralph's 'would you merge it?' positioning. Useful vocabulary confirmation.

market_validation.what: CI-native orchestration around OpenCode — 'smörgåsbord of AI agents' on every merge request.
market_validation.why: Validates CI-native, orchestration-first approach converging with Ralph's completion loop + review stage. Differentiation: Ralph runs locally on your repo, not requiring Cloudflare infrastructure.

unattended_coding_agent.note: No longer an emerging concept; it's an established tool category with competing implementations. Claude Code Auto Mode GA, OvernightAgent, kodo, Symphony, Claude Code Routines all shipping in this space. Differentiation window is shrinking.

multi_agent_orchestration.note: Category vocabulary hardening: single vs multi-agent review, CI-native orchestration, cloud-hosted unattended pipelines. Ralph's vendor-neutral, local-first positioning becomes the differentiator as platforms absorb the feature surface.

reuse_contract.purpose: Shared market and competitor findings artifact for site updates, positioning checks, and marketing tactics.
